---
title: "Data matching"
output: html_document
date: "2026-06-08"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

```{r}
library(lmerTest)
library(lme4)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggnewscale)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(broom.mixed)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)
library(nnet)
library(patchwork)
library(emmeans)
```

```{r}
# install.packages("readxl")

library(readxl)

df_holmes <- read_excel("/Users/sissili/Desktop/Holmes Data/Holmes 01 Matching.xlsx")
df_holmes_matched <- read_excel("/Users/sissili/Desktop/Holmes Data/Holmes 01 Matched.xlsx")

patients_to_match <- df_holmes %>%
  filter(`PATIENT ID` %in% need_to_match$`PATIENT ID`) %>%
  select(`PATIENT ID`, `Patient Age`, `Sex/Gender...3`, `Race/Ethnicity...4`)

```


```{r}

set.seed(123)

simulated_matches <- patients_to_match %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    simulated_ID = paste0("SIM_", `PATIENT ID`),
    sim_gender = `Sex/Gender...3`,
    sim_age = sample((`Patient Age` - 5):(`Patient Age` + 5), 1),
    sim_race = `Race/Ethnicity...4`
  )

simulated_matches
```

```{r}

df_holmes_tobematched <- read_excel("/Users/sissili/Desktop/Holmes Data/Holmes 01 to_be_matched.xlsx")

unique(df_holmes_tobematched$`Race/Ethnicity`)

```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

simulate_race <- function(race) {
  case_when(
    race %in% c("Asian", "East Asian") ~ "Asian",
    race == "Black" ~ "Black",
    race == "White" ~ "White",
    race %in% c("Latino", "Hispanic") ~ sample(c("Latino", "Hispanic"), 1),
    race == "Hispanic / Latino / Asian (half filipino)" ~ sample(c("Latino", "Filipino"), 1),
    race == "White / Columbian / Italian" ~ sample(c("Columbian", "Italian"), 1),
    race == "White / Hispanic" ~ "Hispanic",
    race == "White / puerto rican" ~ "Puerto Rican",
    race == "Asian / White" ~ "Asian",
    race == "White / Korean" ~ "KRN",
    TRUE ~ race  # fallback: keep as is
  )
}

set.seed(123)

simulated_controls <- df_holmes_tobematched %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    sim_ID = paste0("SIM_", `PATIENT ID`),
    sim_gender = `Sex/Gender`,
    sim_age_gap_2 = sample((`Patient Age` - 2):(`Patient Age` + 2), 1),
    sim_age_gap_5 = sample((`Patient Age` - 5):(`Patient Age` + 5), 1),
    sim_race = simulate_race(`Race/Ethnicity`)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

simulated_controls
```
